step_by_step_thinking: According to the references given, α-ketoglutarate is a crucial intermediate in amino acid metabolism but importantly, when metabolized via the α-ketoglutarate dehydrogenase complex, it forms succinyl CoA, an intermediate critical for hemoglobin production. Hence,
raw_response:
According to the references given, α-ketoglutarate is a crucial intermediate in amino acid metabolism but importantly, when metabolized via the α-ketoglutarate dehydrogenase complex, it forms succinyl CoA, an intermediate critical for hemoglobin production. Hence,
